ELPAC Level 1
Lexile 1000–1300L
DTR | Listen to a Speaker Support an Opinion
LSO-MS7-L1-10001300-MSELA7-BNB-LAT-02 | MS ELA: Grade 7 | Acknowledging and Addressing Opposing Claims | Breaking News Broadcast | Latinx / Latin American Experiences
Sign Off in Spanish
Click 🎧 to listen.
Listen to a conversation between two students. The boy is discussing his opinion about adding a Spanish part to the school news broadcast. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer four questions about the conversation and the evidence the boy gives to support his opinion. As you listen, you may take notes.
Listen to the Conversation
1 QUESTION 1:
Why does the boy mention his abuela?
2 QUESTION 2:
Where did the number about families who speak Spanish at home come from?
3 QUESTION 3:
What does the boy say happened at the school across town?
4 QUESTION 4:
What does the boy mean when he says a broadcast should reach the whole community?
Answer all 4 questions to submit.
Choose the best answer for each question. You may change your answers before submitting.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. Identify why the boy mentions his abuela. Identify where the forty percent figure came from. Identify what he says happened at the school across town. Identify his main point about reaching everyone.
CA ELD: PI.A.3, PI.B.5, PI.B.7, PI.B.8, PII.A.1 | CCSS: W.7.1 | MS ELA: Grade 7
